mnemonics: To remember the 8 common rules of inference (Modus Ponens, Modus Tollens, Hypothetical Syllogism, Disjunctive Syllogism, Addition, Simplification, Conjunction, Resolution), use the mnemonic phrase: 'My Pet Has Dirty And Smelly Clothes, Regularly' where each word's initial corresponds to the rule order.
keyPoints: Logical implication (P → Q) is false ONLY when P is true and Q is false.
Rules of inference are valid argument forms used to construct logical proofs.
Modus Ponens (If P then Q, P, therefore Q) and Modus Tollens (If P then Q, Not Q, therefore Not P) are two fundamental rules.
commonMistakes: Affirming the consequent: Assuming P is true because Q is true in 'If P then Q'.
Denying the antecedent: Assuming Q is false because P is false in 'If P then Q'.
